rubric_items.reasoning: Oracle passed and the evidence supports a genuine, durable fix: the job shows Complete with 1/1 completions, the pod is Completed, and the log contains the calculated Pi value (3.1417316314173163) with exit code 0. The agent's summary confirms all four known issues (image name, serviceAccountName, memory format, RBAC pods permission) were addressed, and the K8s activity (role patch + job delete/apply) matches the exact minimal fix set needed. Only reason not to give full marks: the log head showing the actual diagnosis/edit of the manifest is truncated, so the root-cause editing process isn't directly visible in what's provided, though the outcome is consistent with a real fix rather than a lucky pass.

The visible tail of the log shows a coherent, non-flailing sequence: apply the fixed job, launch a background wait/monitor, poll job/pod status, check logs for the Pi result, then re-confirm the final Job status as Complete. This mirrors the diagnose-then-verify pattern expected for this task. No backtracking, contradictory actions, or wasted trial-and-error are visible in the shown portion.

Commands used are targeted and scoped: kubectl get job/pods, kubectl logs with a specific pod, and (per the mutation count) a role patch plus job delete/apply — exactly matching the task's own suggested fix commands. Mutations were kept to 3 (delete, patch, apply), all directly serving the fix with no broad or destructive operations.

Only 3 mutating calls total against a single namespace, aligning precisely with the minimal fix set (delete stale job, patch RBAC role to add pods, re-apply corrected job manifest). No unrelated resources were touched and no collateral churn is evident.
